title: ChatGPT for Beginners
description: Unlock the power of conversational AI. Learn what ChatGPT is, how it works, and how to write basic prompts to assist you with writing, brainstorming, and daily tasks.

seo_metadata.title: ChatGPT for Beginners Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Learn the basics of ChatGPT. Understand Generative AI and write effective prompts for daily productivity.

learning_outcomes: Understand what Large Language Models (LLMs) are and how they generate text
Navigate the ChatGPT interface and manage conversations
Write clear and effective basic prompts for various tasks
Identify the limitations and ethical considerations of using AI
Use ChatGPT for drafting emails, brainstorming, and summarizing text

lessons.title: What is ChatGPT?
lessons.content:
Large Language Models

ChatGPT is an AI language model developed by OpenAI. It is trained on vast amounts of text data to predict the next word in a sequence, allowing it to generate human-like text based on the input (prompt) it receives.

lessons.title: Limitations and Hallucinations
lessons.content:
AI is Not Perfect

ChatGPT does not 'know' facts; it predicts text. Sometimes it confidently outputs incorrect information, a phenomenon known as an AI hallucination. Always verify important facts.

modules.title: The Art of Prompting
modules.description: How to talk to the AI to get what you want.

lessons.title: Basic Prompt Structure
lessons.content:
Clarity is Key

A good prompt is specific. Instead of saying 'Write an email', say 'Write a polite email to my boss asking for next Friday off due to a family event.'

lessons.title: Assigning Roles
lessons.content:
Act As...

You can tell ChatGPT to act as a specific persona (e.g., 'Act as a senior marketing expert'). This helps the AI adopt the correct tone and vocabulary for your task.

lessons.title: Drafting and Editing
lessons.content:
Your AI Assistant

Paste your rough drafts into ChatGPT and ask it to check for grammar, change the tone to be more professional, or summarize a long article into key bullet points.

lessons.title: Brainstorming and Ideation
lessons.content:
Overcoming Blank Page Syndrome

Use ChatGPT to generate ideas for blog posts, gift ideas, or project names. You can ask for '10 creative titles for a presentation on AI.'

mcqs.question: What does LLM stand for in the context of ChatGPT?
How does ChatGPT primarily generate its responses?

options.content: By querying a database of pre-written answers
By predicting the next most likely word in a sequence

mcqs.question: When an AI confidently presents incorrect information as a fact, what is this called?
Which of the following is an example of a well-structured, specific prompt?

options.content: Write an email about the meeting.
Write a professional email to my team summarizing the attached meeting notes and assigning next steps.

mcqs.question: What is a highly effective technique to get ChatGPT to adopt a specific tone or expertise?

options.content: Tell it to 'Act as [a specific persona]'

mcqs.question: Why is it important to verify important facts generated by ChatGPT?

options.content: Because the AI can hallucinate and generate plausible-sounding falsehoods
Because it is illegal not to
Because the AI doesn't know English well

mcqs.question: If you are experiencing 'Blank Page Syndrome', how can ChatGPT best assist you?

options.content: By generating a list of ideas or an outline to get you started
By creating a final, perfect draft immediately

mcqs.question: Can ChatGPT remember context from earlier in the SAME conversation?

options.content: Yes, it has a 'context window' that allows it to remember recent inputs within the same chat thread
No, every single prompt is treated completely independently
Only if you pay for the premium version
Only if you use specific code words

mcqs.question: When asking ChatGPT to summarize a long text, what is a best practice?

options.content: Provide constraints, such as 'Summarize this in 3 bullet points'
Paste the text a word at a time
Ask it to translate the text first
